requirements.description: Proposals must target next-generation AI agents: autonomous systems powered by large AI language models (e.g., large language models or large multimodal models) that can plan, utilise tools, and perform actions autonomously to achieve specified goals based on high-level instructions.
AI agent design must incorporate structured planning and reasoning methods to manage complex tasks, and agents must be equipped with appropriate validation and monitoring techniques.

Proposals must address real-world applications in Apply AI sectors; the call explicitly references data analytics, coding, healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and autonomous driving as application areas.
Proposals must allocate tasks for cohesion activities with the European Partnership on AI, Data and Robotics (ADRA) and the GenAI4EU central Hub (CSA HORIZON-CL4-2025-03-HUMAN-18).
Proposals must incorporate qualitative and quantitative KPIs, benchmarking, and progress monitoring mechanisms to demonstrate progress.

Communicable results must be shared via the AI-on-demand platform; projects must link to AI Factories and Data Labs resources; results may be validated in Testing and Experiment Facilities and deployed via EDIHs.
Activities must start at TRL 2 and achieve TRL 5 by the end of the project.

Potential (non-exhaustive) research areas include: (a) enhancing AI agent autonomy through advanced self-planning and self-optimisation; (b) innovation in memory-augmented AI agents for long-term reasoning and lifelong learning; (c) advanced multi-agent frameworks for collaborative agents including mixed AI architectures; (d) advancing multimodal reasoning for real-world applications.

To qualify for sector-portfolio-based grant award, proposals should address at least one of the three priority application sectors: healthcare, advanced manufacturing, or in-vehicle autonomous driving.
Proposals should build on or seek collaboration with relevant projects and develop synergies with other relevant international, European, national, or regional initiatives.
